archetype_reasoning: This trader has dominant accuracy in Russia_UKR (91.5%, 972 wins) and Trump_US (94%, 342 wins) domains, but catastrophic performance outside these — only 6.5% accuracy on 'Other' markets with 459 losses. The extreme directionality (0.89) combined with concentrated domain expertise confirms a specialist who has genuine edge in two specific areas but is essentially random or wrong outside them. Volume of resolved trades (1917) across only 4 distinct markets reinforces deep specialization over breadth.
domain_blindspots: Other: catastrophic 6.5% accuracy on 459 losses — near-inverse of correct forecasting, possibly systematic misjudgment of fringe geopolitical markets
domain_summary: This trader has exceptional, high-conviction edge in Russia-Ukraine and Trump/US political markets with combined thousands of winning trades. Outside these two domains, signals should be completely ignored — the 6.5% accuracy in 'Other' is so low it may indicate contrarian signals actually have value when faded.
signal_weight_reasoning: Full weight is justified only for Russia_UKR and Trump_US domains where accuracy exceeds 91-94% across very large sample sizes. The 'Other' domain performance is so poor (6.5%) that signals there should not only be discounted but potentially faded. The recent Harris dropout losses (15 losing positions at ~0.69-0.71 entry) show bleeding into US domains beyond their core competency.

summary: This is an elite domain specialist with one of the largest and most accurate Russia-Ukraine track records on the platform, backed by a realized PnL of $3.65M and a LEGENDARY ELO. The trader's signal in Russia_UKR and Trump_US domains is among the most trustworthy available and should be weighted heavily. However, the catastrophic 'Other' domain performance (6.5% on 459 trades) is a severe warning that this expertise does not generalize — outside core domains, this trader's signal may be actively worth fading. The recent Harris NO run represents an uncharacteristic loss streak and the current zero open-position state warrants monitoring for re-engagement.
